contract.title: FDA approves Retatrutide in 2026?
contract.resolution_criteria:
This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the U.S. Food and Drug Administration formally
approves Retatrutide (LY3437943, Eli Lilly's triple GIP/GLP-1/glucagon receptor
agonist) for any indication by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Approval must be
via formal FDA authorization — a New Drug Application (NDA) or Biologics License
Application (BLA) approval letter. Excludes Emergency Use Authorizations (EUA),
compassionate use designations, tentative approvals, and approvable letters.

Retatrutide targets obesity, type 2 diabetes, and related metabolic conditions.
Phase 3 clinical trial data is expected in 2026, with NDA submission expected no
earlier than late 2026, making a 2026 FDA decision unlikely but possible on
accelerated review pathways.

settlement_entities.name: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research

conditions.label: FDA NDA approval letter
conditions.summary: FDA issues a final NDA approval letter for Retatrutide (LY3437943) for any indication by Dec 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Standard or accelerated approval counts; EUA, tentative, and approvable letters do not.

conditions.label: FDA BLA approval letter
conditions.summary: FDA issues a final BLA approval letter for Retatrutide (LY3437943) for any indication by Dec 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Standard or accelerated approval counts; EUA, tentative, and approvable letters do not.

contract.narrative: Recent regulatory activity targets unrelated areas—state AGs pressuring FDA on flavored e‑cigarettes, Supreme Court briefs on mifepristone access, and a Puerto Rico insurance rule—none affect Retatrutide’s review. No agency policy shifts or political pressure specific to Retatrutide are evident; FDA’s 2026 decision remains pending under standard review.

timeline.title: Attorney General Bonta Urges FDA to Reverse Guidance Easing Sales of Flavored E-Cigarettes - 12.05.2026
timeline.one_line_why: State AG urging FDA on e‑cigarette guidance is unrelated to drug approval decisions for Retatrutide and doesn't change its approval odds or timing.

timeline.title: Attorney General Bonta Urges FDA to Reverse Guidance Easing Sales of Flavored E-Cigarettes
timeline.one_line_why: AG urging FDA to reverse e‑cigarette guidance concerns tobacco policy and is unlikely to affect Retatrutide's NDA/BLA approval odds or timing.

timeline.title: CN-2026-377-AS (Enmendada) - 12.05.2026 - Formulario y Tarifas que Serán Efectivo a Partir del Año Calendario 2027
timeline.regulator: Oficina del Comisionado de Seguros de Puerto Rico
timeline.one_line_why: Puerto Rico insurance/forms-and-fees administrative rule; unrelated to FDA drug approval of Retatrutide, so it does not change approval odds.

timeline.title: Attorney General James Leads Coalition Urging Supreme Court to Protect Access to Mifepristone
timeline.one_line_why: Coalition to protect mifepristone preserves FDA authority broadly but has no material, direct effect on Retatrutide approval likelihood or timing.

timeline.title: Attorney General Bonta Urged U.S. Supreme Court to Protect Telehealth Access to Mifepristone - May 4, 2026
timeline.one_line_why: This AG filing concerns telehealth access to mifepristone and is unrelated to Lilly, Retatrutide, or FDA NDA/BLA approval timing.

primary_drivers: AG letters urging FDA to reverse flavored e‑cigarette guidance
Coalition/Supreme Court filings to protect access (telehealth/other)
Regulatory filing CN-2026-377-AS re: forms and fees
heat_panel.explainer: Dormant heat reflects a small cluster of attorney general advocacy letters (Bonta, James) plus a single regulatory filing (CN-2026-377-AS); overall activity has cooled this week.
